other_observers: I was by myself.; I live only a mile from that location and visit it each spring until I find Lesser Nighthawks. Once I spread the work many visit the site to see the birds. I am not sure who all came. Cole Wild and his friends were here this summer as well as a Canadian birders.
specific_location: One mile west of Nucla on West 5th Avenue at the sewer lagoons.
description: Nighthawk had short rounded wings and wingbar nearer the tip than the Common Nighthawks it was flying with. The leading primary wing feather on both wings were shorter than the second feather which was the longest of all primary wing feathers. The overall color of the nighthawk was buff. In Common Nighthawks there where speckles of white but in this nighthawk the speckles were tan or buff colored. Even the wingbars were an off white. There was some molt in the secondary wing feathers. The tail of this nighthawk was a little shorter than the Common's tail. It was a male because of buff banding on the tail and buff throat patch.Behaviors: The nighthawks was flying around the sewer lagoons feeding on insects with many Common Nighthawks. Several times the nighthawk passed right overhead and gave me close up views. The flight pattern of the Common Nighthawks when feeding were different than this nighthawk.Call: nonePlumage: adult
